attributes.description: Alerts when `mongodb.cursor.timeout.count` increases. Cursor timeouts mean clients can't consume result batches in time.
attributes.name: [MongoDB OTel] Cursor timeouts

investigation_guide.blob:
## MongoDB cursor timeouts

### What fired
`mongodb.cursor.timeout.count` increased during the window.

### Why it matters
MongoDB cursors expire after 10 minutes of inactivity. Timeouts mean the application did not iterate fast enough or held the cursor without use.

### Triage
1. Check application query patterns and batch sizes.
2. Look for cursor leaks.

### Remediation
- Fix application to iterate cursors promptly or use `noCursorTimeout` carefully.

### Tuning
- Fires on any increase.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-mongodbreceiver.otel-default
// Scope to MongoDB OTel metrics
| WHERE data_stream.dataset == "mongodbreceiver.otel"
| WHERE metrics.mongodb.cursor.timeout.count IS NOT NULL
// Delta over time window (metric is non-monotonic sum; increase indicates new timeouts)
| STATS min_val = MIN(metrics.mongodb.cursor.timeout.count), max_val = MAX(metrics.mongodb.cursor.timeout.count) BY resource.attributes.server.address
| EVAL new_timeouts = max_val - min_val
// Alert on any new cursor timeouts (clients unable to consume results in time)
| WHERE new_timeouts > 0
| SORT new_timeouts DESC
| LIMIT 20
